title: tephrosia subaphylla
intro: Tephrosia subaphylla, also known as the 'Cassia', is a flowering plant species native to various parts of Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to help soothe skin irritations and combat infections. Tephrosia subaphylla is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
